danger_signals.visual_form: Garbage blocks accumulating in player's grid
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent is winning; player is in immediate danger of grid overflow

danger_signals.visual_form: Puyos ascending toward top of play field
danger_signals.meaning: Game state approaching loss condition; imminent failure if blocks reach ceiling

danger_signals.visual_form: Failed match attempts; puyos not connecting
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of offensive capability; vulnerability to opponent counterattack

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple puyos disappearing in cascade; screen flash or visual feedback
safety_signals.meaning: Offensive action successful; sending garbage blocks to opponent

safety_signals.visual_form: Empty or mostly empty play field with room at top
safety_signals.meaning: Player has safety margin; time to build strategy without immediate loss risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Player chains while opponent's garbage blocks incoming
safety_signals.meaning: Player can neutralize threat through counter-attack; defensive option available

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Story mode positions player as hero fighting against Dark Prince's plans; world-saving narrative

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dark Prince archetype; villain seeking world domination
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Final boss encounter after defeating 12 sequential opponents
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Represents existential threat to world order; defeated in final story progression

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Various character types from Madō Monogatari universe
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Non-aligned with Dark Prince (except Rulue, who has personal motivation)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Encountered in fixed sequence during story mode; represent obstacles rather than coordinated faction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Falling puyos; balanced grid fill on both sides; normal block descent speed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players exchanging turns; building chains; stable match state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cascade animations; opponent's grid filling with garbage blocks; visual feedback of combo success
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponent grid rising; opponent defensive; game pressure shifting toward player advantage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Play field blocks approaching ceiling; reduced empty space; visual crowding
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must clear blocks urgently; limited strategic options; reactive play mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sequential opponent encounters; character roster displayed; final encounter with Dark Prince
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fixed opponent sequence (12 opponents before final boss); narrative progression through combat victories

environmental_storytelling.example: Story mode introduces 12 opponents before Dark Prince; each represents obstacle to world-saving goal; Rulue specifically noted as motivated by romance rather than Dark Prince allegiance
Arle Nadja presented as 16-year-old spellcaster standing against Dark Prince's world domination plans; framed as hero required to 'foil' villain's ambitions
Victory in story mode results in world being 'saved' and protagonist returning home; implies stakes are existential and binary (world saved or conquered)
Characters drawn from Madō Monogatari (1990 RPG); establishes shared world context; puyos themselves are fictional creatures with implied lore

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Continues building chains; offensive mode; sending garbage blocks
Defensive play; attempting to clear blocks before grid overflow; reactive rather than strategic
Fixed opponent in final story sequence; requires defeating after 12 preliminary matches
Sequential fixed battles; story progression gates; not coordinated faction action

notes: Puyo Puyo is a puzzle game with minimal faction-based world-state communication. Political situation (Dark Prince's threat to world domination) is communicated through narrative framing rather than visual systems. Danger and safety signals are primarily mechanical (grid fill state, chain success/failure) rather than thematic. The game uses character encounter sequencing as primary storytelling method. Visual communication of world-state centers on play field mechanics rather than environmental or systemic visual indicators of political/faction conditions. Story mode provides narrative context that frames puzzle victories as world-saving actions.
